task_completion.description: Create a 'Wide' mask with a threshold of -200 to 3071 HU
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius GUI (Masks list or threshold panel), a mask is visible with the threshold range set from -200 to 3071. It may be named 'Wide'.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Bone' mask with a threshold of 226 to 3071 HU
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second mask is visible in the Masks list with the threshold range set from 226 to 3071. It may be named 'Bone'.

task_completion.description: Generate a 'Soft Tissue' mask by subtracting the Bone mask from the Wide mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Boolean operations panel is used with the 'Subtraction' or 'Difference' operation selected between the Wide and Bone masks, resulting in a third mask (e.g., 'Soft Tissue') appearing in the masks list.

task_completion.description: Create a 3D surface from the 'Soft Tissue' mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D surface is generated and visible in the 3D viewer pane or the 'Surfaces' list, showing the soft tissue (skin/muscle/brain) with the skull/bone removed.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as a binary STL file to /home/ga/Documents/soft_tissue_only.stl
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' dialog is visible showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/soft_tissue_only.stl' with the binary STL format selected, or a file manager shows the successfully created file.
A 'Save Project' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/boolean_masks.inv3', or the InVesalius application title bar updates to show 'boolean_masks.inv3'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the InVesalius 3 interface (thresholding, boolean operations, exporting) rather than just running terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The 3D surface generated is a genuine result of the boolean subtraction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer shows a medically accurate soft tissue structure (head shape without the bright skull bone), proving the boolean subtraction was actually performed.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's save/export dialogs to create the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialogs for the STL and INV3 files are visible in the screenshots, ensuring the files weren't just created as empty dummy files via the terminal (e.g., using 'touch').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius 3. The 'Wide' mask must use a minimum threshold of -200 HU. The 'Bone' mask must use a minimum threshold of 226 HU (standard bone threshold). At least 3 masks should be created in total. The final STL must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/soft_tissue_only.stl' and the project to '/home/ga/Documents/boolean_masks.inv3'.
